feed.title: BitisGabonica
feed.subtitle: A simple blog for posting CTF writeups, guides, walkthroughs and other technical stuff

entry.title: Unicode writeup
entry.summary: Summary This box focuses on exploiting an authentication system using a jwt with an insecure jku parameter. After this, we can do unicode normalization to gain lfi, which will allow us to get a password. We can then get the root flag by reversing a python binary. Foothold We start out by doing an nmap port scan: # Nmap 7.92 scan initiated Sat Mar 5 14:59:22 2022 as: nmap -sC -sV -Pn -o nmap/i...

entry.title: Undetected writeup
entry.summary: Summary This is a box that focuses on reversing a lot of files from a previous attacker who has been so kind as to leave his backdoors in place for us. Let’s take a look! Foothold We start out by doing an nmap port scan: # Nmap 7.92 scan initiated Sat Mar 5 16:44:36 2022 as: nmap -sC -sV -p- -o nmap/full.txt 10.129.151.107 Nmap scan report for 10.129.151.107 Host is up (0.047s latency). Not s...

entry.title: Pinned writeup
entry.summary: Description This app has stored my credentials and I can only login automatically. I tried to intercept the login request and restore my password, but this seems to be a secure connection. Can you help bypass this security restriction and intercept the password in plaintext? Setup and flag I used genymotion to emulate an android device. Install necessary tools with the commands pip install Fr...

entry.title: APKrypt writeup
entry.summary: Description Can you get the ticket without the VIP code? Writeup We receive an apk file, which we can then decompile via codium and the APKLab extension. The MainActivity.java file is seen below: package com.example.apkrypt; import android.app.Activity; import android.os.Bundle; import android.util.Base64; import android.view.View; import android.widget.Button; import android.widget.EditText;...

entry.title: Spectra writeup
entry.summary: Summary This was an easy box that focused on enumerating a wordpress site, getting access as admin and then installing a malicious php plugin that gave a reverse shell. Once in on the box, a password for another user could be found which had sudo rights to run initctl. This could be exploited by changing the config file into running arbitrary commands. Foothold We start out by checking out the ...
